commands.repair_reason: Human maintainer review is needed for the protected label, compatibility-sensitive Doctor repair sequencing, stack order, and missing live CLI proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: The current diff only touches Doctor TypeScript source/tests and does not include dependency, lockfile, workflow, secret, or package-resolution changes. commands.review_summary:
The PR exposes legacy sandbox registry files as a structured Doctor health check with repair effects while filtering that check out of the broad structured repair pass so the existing positional migration remains the mutating path.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +138. Total +217 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR for structured Doctor reporting rather than a bug report. Source review confirms current main lacks the structured sandbox registry check, while the PR diff adds it.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is needed for the protected label, compatibility-sensitive Doctor repair sequencing, stack order, and missing live CLI proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: Doctor: expose sandbox registry findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes legacy sandbox registry files as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps the real registry migration on the existing positional doctor:sandbox flow used by openclaw doctor --fix.
- Skips the sandbox registry check in the broad structured repair bridge so this PR does not duplicate or reorder the existing migration.

This is the small version of the sandbox doctor slice. It does not move Docker image builds, namespace probes, or sandbox scope notes into structured repair yet. The existing registry migration flow now shares its detector with the health registry, and dry-run reports whether doctor would migrate, remove, or quarantine the legacy registry file.

## Verification

- pnpm install
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-sandbox.ts src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-sandbox.ts src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot - 4 files, 49 tests
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- bash /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main - clean after fixing the accepted empty-registry effect finding

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openclaw doctor --lint can report legacy sandbox registry files, and dry-run repair output can describe the state operation without taking over the real registry migration.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0, OpenClaw source checkout based on current origin/main.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused sandbox/doctor Vitest files, focused oxfmt/oxlint, core tsgo, core test tsgo, and autoreview as listed above.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed with 4 files and 49 tests; both tsgo lanes exited 0; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings after the empty-registry dry-run effect was fixed.
Observed result after fix: sandbox registry files are registered as core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files; lint/dry-run can see structured findings/effects, while broad structured fix skips this id so the existing positional sandbox migration remains the only real mutating path.
What was not tested: a live sandbox registry migration against a real user state directory was not run because this PR preserves the existing migration command path and only adds structured reporting around its detector.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
